usa.name: United States
usa.notes: Traffic laws vary by state. Federal minimums apply. Fines listed are typical ranges.

general_rules.license_requirements: Written test + road test. Graduated licensing for teens (learner's permit at 15-16, provisional at 16-17). Real ID compliant license required for federal facilities.
general_rules.right_of_way: Yield to pedestrians at crosswalks. Right turn on red allowed (unless posted). Four-way stop: first to arrive goes first.
general_rules.child_safety: Car seats required for children under 4. Booster seats until age 8 or 4'9". Children under 13 must sit in rear seat.

speeding.name: Speeding
speeding.description: Exceeding posted speed limits. Fines vary by how much over the limit.
speeding.safety_advice: Speed limits are set for safety. Fines increase with speed and in school/work zones.

drunk_driving.name: DUI / DWI (Driving Under Influence)
drunk_driving.description: Operating a vehicle with BAC of 0.08% or higher (0.04% for commercial, 0.02% for under-21)
drunk_driving.safety_advice: Zero tolerance for underage drinking and driving. Use ride-sharing services.

no_seatbelt.name: Not Wearing Seat Belt
no_seatbelt.description: Driver or passenger not wearing a seat belt
additional_penalties: Points on license in some states
no_seatbelt.safety_advice: Seat belts reduce fatal crash risk by 45%. Click it or ticket.

signal_jump.name: Running a Red Light
signal_jump.description: Failing to stop at a red traffic signal
additional_penalties: Traffic school option in some states
signal_jump.safety_advice: Red light cameras are widely used. Always stop at red lights.

using_phone_while_driving.name: Distracted Driving (Phone Use)
using_phone_while_driving.description: Using handheld mobile phone while driving
using_phone_while_driving.safety_advice: Hands-free is the law in most states. Put your phone down while driving.

no_insurance.name: Driving Without Insurance
no_insurance.description: Operating a motor vehicle without minimum liability insurance
no_insurance.safety_advice: Auto insurance is mandatory in almost all states. Minimum liability coverage is required.

no_helmet.name: Motorcycle Without Helmet
no_helmet.description: Riding motorcycle without DOT-approved helmet (law varies by state — 18 states require for all riders, 29 for minors only)
no_helmet.safety_advice: Helmets reduce fatality risk by 37%. Always wear one regardless of state law.

no_license.name: Driving Without a Valid License
no_license.description: Operating motor vehicle without valid driver's license
no_license.safety_advice: Always carry your valid license while driving.

reckless_driving.name: Reckless Driving
reckless_driving.description: Driving with willful disregard for safety (weaving, street racing, excessive speed)
reckless_driving.safety_advice: Reckless driving is a criminal offense in most states.

uk.name: United Kingdom
uk.notes: UK uses a penalty points system. Accumulating 12 points in 3 years leads to disqualification.

general_rules.license_requirements: Theory test + practical driving test. Provisional license at 17. Points system: 12 points = disqualification.
general_rules.right_of_way: Drive on the LEFT. Yield at roundabouts to traffic from the right. Zebra crossings: always stop for pedestrians.
general_rules.child_safety: Child car seats required until 12 years or 135 cm tall. Rear-facing seats for babies under 15 months.

speeding.name: Speeding
speeding.description: Exceeding the speed limit on any road
speeding.safety_advice: National speed limit: 30 mph urban, 60 mph single carriageway, 70 mph motorway.

drunk_driving.name: Drink Driving
drunk_driving.description: Driving with alcohol level above the legal limit
drunk_driving.safety_advice: Don't drink and drive. Even one drink can impair your reaction time.

no_seatbelt.name: Not Wearing a Seatbelt
no_seatbelt.description: Driver or passenger not wearing a seat belt when required
no_seatbelt.safety_advice: You must wear a seat belt in all vehicles. Exemptions are very limited.

using_phone_while_driving.name: Using Mobile Phone While Driving
using_phone_while_driving.description: Using a hand-held phone while driving or riding
using_phone_while_driving.safety_advice: Even hands-free can be distracting. Pull over safely if you need to use your phone.

signal_jump.name: Running a Red Light
signal_jump.description: Failing to comply with traffic light signals
signal_jump.safety_advice: Red means stop. Amber means stop unless unsafe to do so.

no_insurance.name: Driving Without Insurance
no_insurance.description: Using a motor vehicle on a road without valid insurance
no_insurance.safety_advice: Third-party insurance is the legal minimum in the UK.

no_helmet.name: Motorcycle Without Helmet
no_helmet.description: Riding a motorcycle without an approved safety helmet (Road Traffic Act 1988, Section 16)
additional_penalties: No penalty points but fixed penalty notice
no_helmet.safety_advice: Helmet is mandatory for all motorcycle riders and passengers in the UK.

no_license.name: Driving Without a Licence
no_license.description: Driving without a valid, full or provisional driving licence
no_license.safety_advice: You must have a valid licence for the category of vehicle you are driving.

uae.name: United Arab Emirates
uae.notes: UAE has strict traffic enforcement with Salik cameras and radar. Black points system in effect.

general_rules.license_requirements: Driving school mandatory for new applicants. International driving permits accepted for tourists (up to 6 months). UAE license for residents.
general_rules.right_of_way: Drive on the RIGHT. Roundabouts: yield to traffic already on the roundabout. UAE has strict lane discipline enforcement.
general_rules.child_safety: Children under 4 must use car seats. Children under 10 must sit in rear seat.

speeding.name: Exceeding Speed Limit
speeding.description: Exceeding the posted speed limit. Fine varies with speed over limit.
additional_penalties: Vehicle confiscation for 60 days at 60+ km/h over
speeding.safety_advice: Speed limits are strictly enforced by radar. 120 km/h max on highways.

drunk_driving.name: Driving Under Influence of Alcohol
drunk_driving.description: Zero tolerance policy - any amount of alcohol while driving is illegal
drunk_driving.safety_advice: UAE has ZERO tolerance for alcohol while driving. Even residual traces can lead to prosecution.

no_seatbelt.name: Not Wearing Seat Belt
no_seatbelt.description: Driver or passengers not wearing seat belts
no_seatbelt.safety_advice: All occupants must wear seat belts at all times.

signal_jump.name: Jumping Red Signal
signal_jump.safety_advice: Red light cameras are widespread. Heavy penalties apply.

using_phone_while_driving.name: Using Mobile Phone While Driving
using_phone_while_driving.description: Using a mobile phone while driving without hands-free
using_phone_while_driving.safety_advice: Use Bluetooth or hands-free system only.

no_helmet.name: Motorcycle Without Helmet
no_helmet.description: Riding a motorcycle without an approved safety helmet
no_helmet.safety_advice: Helmets are mandatory for all motorcycle riders in the UAE.

no_license.name: Driving Without a Valid License
no_license.description: Driving without a valid UAE license or international driving permit
no_license.safety_advice: Residents must obtain a UAE license. Tourists can use IDP for up to 6 months.

reckless_driving.name: Reckless / Dangerous Driving
reckless_driving.description: Endangering lives through dangerous or reckless driving behavior
reckless_driving.safety_advice: Reckless driving in the UAE can result in deportation for expats.

germany.name: Germany
germany.notes: Germany uses a points system (Flensburg). Autobahn has advisory speed of 130 km/h but no general speed limit.

general_rules.license_requirements: Theory + practical test. First-aid course mandatory. Minimum age 18 (17 with supervised driving). Probation period of 2 years.
general_rules.right_of_way: Priority to the right (Rechts vor Links) at unmarked intersections. Trams always have priority. Emergency vehicles must be given way via Rettungsgasse.
general_rules.child_safety: Children under 12 or under 150 cm must use approved child restraint. Rear-facing seats mandatory until 15 months.

speeding.name: Speeding (Geschwindigkeitsüberschreitung)
speeding.description: Exceeding the posted speed limit. Penalties escalate with speed over limit.
additional_penalties: 1 month driving ban at 31+ km/h over in urban areas
speeding.safety_advice: Inside towns: 50 km/h. Outside: 100 km/h. Autobahn advisory: 130 km/h.

drunk_driving.name: Drink Driving (Trunkenheit am Steuer)
drunk_driving.description: Driving with BAC above 0.05% (0% for probationary licence holders under 21)
drunk_driving.safety_advice: Zero tolerance for new drivers. Any alcohol increases crash risk.

no_seatbelt.name: Not Wearing Seat Belt
no_seatbelt.description: Driver or passenger not wearing a seat belt
no_seatbelt.safety_advice: Seat belts are mandatory for all vehicle occupants.

signal_jump.name: Running a Red Light
signal_jump.description: Failing to stop at a red traffic light
additional_penalties: 1 month driving ban if red for more than 1 second
signal_jump.safety_advice: Running a red light after 1+ seconds carries much heavier penalties.

using_phone_while_driving.name: Using Mobile Phone While Driving
using_phone_while_driving.description: Using a handheld device while driving
using_phone_while_driving.safety_advice: Hands-free use is permitted but discouraged.

no_helmet.name: Motorcycle Without Helmet
no_helmet.safety_advice: Helmets are mandatory. Even a small fine, but huge insurance implications.

no_license.name: Driving Without a License (Fahren ohne Fahrerlaubnis)
no_license.description: Criminal offense (not just administrative) — up to 1 year imprisonment or fine determined by court
no_license.safety_advice: This is a CRIMINAL offense in Germany, not just a traffic violation.

australia.name: Australia
australia.notes: Traffic laws vary by state/territory. Demerit points system used nationally. Fines are in AUD.

general_rules.license_requirements: Graduated licensing: Learner (L) → Provisional P1 → Provisional P2 → Full. Knowledge test + driving test + hazard perception test.
general_rules.right_of_way: Drive on the LEFT. Give way to vehicles on roundabouts. Hook turns for right-turning cyclists in Melbourne.
general_rules.child_safety: Rear-facing seats until 6 months. Forward-facing until 4 years. Booster seat until 7 years.

speeding.name: Speeding
speeding.description: Exceeding the speed limit. Penalties increase with speed over limit.
speeding.safety_advice: School zones (40 km/h) are heavily enforced. Double demerits on public holidays.

drunk_driving.name: Drink Driving
drunk_driving.description: Driving with BAC above 0.05% (zero for L and P platers)
drunk_driving.safety_advice: Random breath testing (RBT) is very common. Don't risk it.

no_seatbelt.name: Not Wearing Seat Belt
no_seatbelt.description: Driver or passenger not wearing seat belt
no_seatbelt.safety_advice: All occupants must wear seat belts. Child restraints required for children.

using_phone_while_driving.name: Using Mobile Phone While Driving
using_phone_while_driving.description: Using a hand-held mobile phone while driving
using_phone_while_driving.safety_advice: Phone detection cameras are active. Fines are doubled in school zones.

signal_jump.name: Running a Red Light
signal_jump.description: Failing to stop at a red traffic signal
signal_jump.safety_advice: Red light cameras operate at intersections across all states.

no_helmet.name: Not Wearing Bicycle/Motorcycle Helmet
no_helmet.description: Riding bicycle or motorcycle without approved helmet (mandatory for ALL riders in Australia)
no_helmet.safety_advice: Australia is one of few countries requiring helmets for cyclists too.

no_license.name: Driving Unlicensed
no_license.description: Driving without holding a valid licence for the class of vehicle
no_license.safety_advice: Always carry your licence. Licence must match the vehicle class.

canada.name: Canada
canada.notes: Provincial laws vary. Demerit points system. Criminal Code applies to serious offenses like impaired driving.

general_rules.license_requirements: Graduated licensing in all provinces: G1 (learner) → G2 (novice) → G (full). Knowledge test + road tests.
general_rules.right_of_way: Drive on the RIGHT. Right turn on red allowed (except Montreal). Yield to school buses with flashing lights.
general_rules.child_safety: Rear-facing seats until 2 years. Forward-facing until 18 kg. Booster until 36 kg or 145 cm.

speeding.name: Speeding
additional_penalties: License suspension for stunt driving (50+ km/h over)
speeding.safety_advice: Urban limits: 50 km/h. Highway: 100-110 km/h. School zones: 30-40 km/h.

drunk_driving.name: Impaired Driving
drunk_driving.description: Operating a vehicle while impaired by alcohol or drugs (BAC 0.08%+)
drunk_driving.safety_advice: Impaired driving is a Criminal Code offense in Canada with very severe consequences.

using_phone_while_driving.name: Distracted Driving
using_phone_while_driving.description: Using a hand-held electronic device while driving
using_phone_while_driving.safety_advice: All provinces ban hand-held phone use. Some ban all phone use for novice drivers.

no_seatbelt.name: Not Wearing Seat Belt
no_seatbelt.description: Driver or passenger failing to wear seat belt
no_seatbelt.safety_advice: All occupants must wear seat belts. Driver is responsible for passengers under 16.

signal_jump.name: Running a Red Light
signal_jump.description: Failing to stop at a red traffic light
signal_jump.safety_advice: Red light cameras are common in major cities.

no_helmet.name: Motorcycle Without Helmet
no_helmet.description: Riding motorcycle without approved helmet (mandatory in all provinces)
no_helmet.safety_advice: Helmets are mandatory for all motorcycle riders and passengers in Canada.

no_license.name: Driving Without Valid Licence
no_license.description: Driving without a valid licence for the class of vehicle
no_license.safety_advice: Always carry your licence and ensure it's valid for your vehicle class.
